answer:
**In the *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, written documents (*lekhya*) are established as a core element of *vyavahāra* (legal procedure), one of the 18 topics of dispute, and a primary form of human proof alongside witnesses and possession.**[^1][^2] The text provides extensive rules for documents' creation, validation (e.g., signatures, scribes, witnesses), kinds (e.g., royal, partition, debt), and flaws (e.g., forgery, poor script), emphasizing their enduring value even if witnesses die.[^3][^4] Human proof (*mānusam pramāṇam*) is prioritized over divine (*daivam pramāṇam*, like ordeals), and documents prevail when witnesses are unavailable or conflicted.[^5]

No operative claim on writing as evidence appears in Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation* (Vols. I–IV). The work analyzes legislation's effects on society, morals, and economy but does not address evidentiary standards, testimony, or documents in legal proof. Searches for relevant terms (e.g., "writing," "document," "evidence," "témoignage") yield no matches in English translations.

**Neither tradition explicitly compares writing's reliability to oral testimony (*sākṣi*).** The *Bṛhaspati Smṛti* treats both as equivalent human proofs under scrutiny (witnesses admonished for truth; documents checked for forgery), with no hierarchy stated—though documents offer permanence (*Brh_1,6.45*: "A document that is shown... succeeds everywhere, even when the witnesses are dead").[^6] Comte's silence precludes comparison.

[^1]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [¶ p-cb5a36](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-cb5a36).  
[^2]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [¶ p-510297](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-510297) (three human proofs).  
[^3]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [¶¶ p-dc2d5a–p-6f3110](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-dc2d5a) (document rules).  
[^4]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [¶ p-a9b952](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-a9b952) (flaws).  
[^5]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [¶ p-794a7a](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-794a7a); [¶ p-fc9858](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-fc9858).  
[^6]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1, [¶ p-6f3110](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-6f3110).
